ddls:
CREATE TABLE "addresses" (
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address */,
  "id_address_number" TEXT /* Custom identifier for the address, often used in internal systems */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the address (e.g., client name, warehouse name) */,
  "address_type_id" INTEGER /* Type of the address (e.g., delivery point, warehouse, client location) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the address */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the address */,
  "street" TEXT /* Street name and number */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the address */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the address */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the address */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the address */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the address was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the address was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the address */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the address */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_address_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_type_id") REFERENCES address_types ("address_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_types" (
  "address_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address type */,
  "address_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the address type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the address type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "distance_calculations" (
  "distance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each distance calculation */,
  "address_id_a" INTEGER /* ID of the first address in the pair */,
  "address_id_b" INTEGER /* ID of the second address in the pair */,
  "distance" REAL /* Distance between the two addresses */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the distance (e.g., kilometers, miles) */,
  "calculated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the distance was calculated */,
  "calculated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the calculation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_distance_calculations_address_id_b FOREIGN KEY ("address_id_b") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_distance_calculations_address_id_a FOREIGN KEY ("address_id_a") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_distance_calculations_calculated_by FOREIGN KEY ("calculated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., dispatcher, driver, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "distance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the distance calculation being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the distance calculation */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the distance calculation was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_distance_id FOREIGN KEY ("distance_id") REFERENCES distance_calculations ("distance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "routes" (
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each route */,
  "route_name" TEXT /* Name of the route */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the route was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the route was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("route_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "route_addresses" (
  "route_id" INTEGER /* ID of the route */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* ID of the address in the route */,
  "sequence" INTEGER /* Sequence of the address in the route */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("route_id", "address_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicles" (
  "vehicle_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vehicle */,
  "vehicle_number" TEXT /* Vehicle number */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Capacity of the vehicle */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the vehicle was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the vehicle was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vehicle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicle_routes" (
  "vehicle_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vehicle */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* ID of the route assigned to the vehicle */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date when the route was assigned to the vehicle */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vehicle_id", "route_id")
)
